Arrange in correct logical order:

B: It is especially suitable for families because no club membership is needed.
C: Walking and contact with the living world are important parts of growing up.
D: It is a natural activity that costs little and gives enjoyment.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: It may have as many as 40 or 50 buckets, each capable of raising 1 cubic metre of spoil.
B: A dredger of this kind can raise about 40,000 cubic metres of spoil in a week.
C: A bucket dredger is useful because it can dredge accurately in channels and harbours.
D: If the buckets have cutting teeth, the dredger can also cut and raise harder material.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Arsine, a poisonous gas compound of arsenic and hydrogen, is used in some electronic processes.
B: The compounds of arsenic are mostly poisonous.
C: Among the important commercial compounds is arsenious oxide, also called white arsenic.
D: Arsenic acid, lead arsenate and calcium arsenate have also been used in agriculture.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: When the wax is removed, only the areas that were not waxed are coloured.
B: The cloth is then dyed and dried.
C: Instead of painting colour directly on cloth, wax is used to protect selected areas.
D: The process used for batik is called resist dyeing.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: The word friction comes from a Latin word meaning “rub”.
B: Friction occurs when two surfaces rub or move against each other.
C: This resistance, or force opposing motion, is called friction.
D: When a heavy wooden box is pushed along the floor, resistance acts between the box and the floor.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: The eel-like hagfish has a very unpleasant way of life.
B: More than 100 hagfish have been found inside one large dead fish.
C: If the host fish was alive originally, it slowly dies as its body is eaten away.
D: The hagfish bores into other fish and feeds from inside their bodies.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: The laws were written in the Sumerian language using wedge-shaped cuneiform letters.
B: Hammurabi was concerned with bringing order and justice to his kingdom.
C: This code contained more than 280 laws inscribed on a large stone pillar.
D: He set up a strong central government and gathered the laws into a single code.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Australia is larger, but it is not usually counted as an island.
B: More than four-fifths of Greenland is covered by a vast ice cap.
C: Greenland is about 2,670 km from north to south and over 1,050 km wide at its widest point.
D: Greenland is the world’s largest island and lies in the North Atlantic Ocean.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: A medicine such as paracetamol may help lower fever or relieve aches.
B: Whooping cough and some other infections are caused by bacteria and may need different treatment.
C: Chickenpox, measles, German measles and mumps are all caused by viruses.
D: Few drugs work directly against viruses, so rest and supportive care are often important.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: With cement, people can build dams, foundations, bridges and high-rise buildings.
B: For this reason, cement is used in many forms of construction.
C: Cement is one of the most versatile binders known to people.
D: It is also used to make building blocks, roofing tiles and many other products.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: This claim is doubtful.
B: Paintings from the eighteenth century show members of the same clan wearing different tartans.
C: The oldest known painting showing Highland dress dates from about 1660, and earlier records are few.
D: It is sometimes claimed that Scottish tartans identified different clans in ancient times.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: The body of the common African civet is about 90 cm long.
B: Its coarse grey fur is tinged with yellow and marked with black spots and bands.
C: As a rule, the civet lives in a hole in the ground and comes out mostly at night to search for food.
D: The civets of India and other parts of Asia are usually smaller than the African civet.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Nowadays, many people camp comfortably using caravans, campers or well-equipped tents.
B: Tents have been used by people for thousands of years.
C: Explorers and mountain climbers have camped in remote and difficult places.
D: They are still the main homes for some wandering peoples, such as the Bedouin.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Slow-speed film reacts slowly to light and is used in very bright conditions.
B: Film comes in different speeds, shown by ASA, ISO or DIN numbers.
C: Light passing through a camera lens acts on light-sensitive chemicals in the film.
D: Medium-speed film is used in normal light, while fast-speed film is used in dimmer conditions.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: The state has been made more habitable by water, natural gas and hydroelectric power.
B: Most of its landscape consists of rugged mountains, arid desert, mesas and buttes.
C: More than 300 mountain ranges cross the state from north to south.
D: Nevada is one of the driest and hottest states in the United States.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: At intervals along the mycelium, fruiting bodies called sporophores develop.
B: Mushrooms spread by spores that develop on the gills.
C: These spores grow into a network of underground threads called mycelium.
D: When the spores ripen, they are released and may grow if they land in a warm, moist place.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Some people are born with greater possibilities, or potential intelligence, than others.
B: Intelligence is improved by learning.
C: Intelligence is no longer thought of as one single general quality behind all behaviour.
D: However, potential intelligence may not develop unless it is encouraged and stimulated.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: This payment usually amounts to about £16,000.
B: Even so, some former MPs struggle to make ends meet.
C: On top of this, they receive a winding-up allowance for unpaid staff and office costs.
D: MPs receive leaving payments, sometimes called golden handshakes, when they leave office.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: These uses include, for example, diamonds in a dentist’s drill.
B: The remaining diamonds are used for industrial rather than decorative purposes.
C: Other industrial diamonds are used in engineering tools that need very hard cutting edges.
D: Less than half of rough diamonds are suitable for cutting into jewellery.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Slowly, differences in speech become more marked and may develop into dialects.
B: Dialects begin when people who once spoke together become separated from one another.
C: Language changes as it is passed from parents to children.
D: Separation may happen because of mountains, rivers, distance or other barriers.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: The work done to bring about an energy change is equal to the quantity of energy changed.
B: The total energy after any change is the same as the total energy before the change.
C: This important principle is known as the conservation of energy.
D: Energy may change from one form to another, and work is the process that causes the change.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Many endangered species reproduce slowly and cannot quickly replace the numbers they lose.
B: It is thought that one species becomes extinct every day, while many more become threatened.
C: Biologists estimate that one in ten species is in danger of dying out.
D: That estimate means there are at least a million endangered species of plants and animals.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Sometimes wages are based on time, such as a fixed rate per hour or per week.
B: Such payments are usually controlled by law so that workers are protected from unfair treatment.
C: Wages, or salaries, are payments made by employers to employees in return for work.
D: Wages are usually paid in money, but sometimes they include payments in kind.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Around the world, techniques for measuring pupil development and achievement have improved.
B: Standardized tests are now used to a great extent.
C: These tests compare a student with classmates and with wider achievement standards.
D: Greater attention to individual differences has led to better methods of assessment.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: This relationship is known as the circular flow of income.
B: In economic life, people are dependent on one another.
C: Whenever somebody spends money, somebody else earns money.
D: Macroeconomics studies how the whole economy of a country works.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: In fact, the word “echinoderm” means “spiny-skinned”.
B: In sea urchins, these spines are especially obvious.
C: In many echinoderms, the plates have knobs or spines that stick through the skin.
D: The skeleton is made of hard, chalky plates just under the skin.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: The oceanic crust is only about 6–8 km thick.
B: There are two main kinds of crust.
C: The continental crust averages about 35 km thick and is even thicker under high mountains.
D: One kind is oceanic crust beneath the oceans, and the other is continental crust beneath land.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Sound waves cannot be seen or felt, but the ear can detect very small vibrations.
B: Only when the signals reach the brain do we hear anything.
C: The hammer, anvil and stirrup of the middle ear take up the vibrations and pass them onward.
D: Sound is first caught by the outer part of the ear and sent inward.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: Dyslexia tends to run in families and is more common in males than females.
B: Most dyslexic people also have problems with spelling.
C: People with dyslexia have trouble seeing the differences between words.
D: They may reverse letters, such as reading “saw” as “was”, or mix up similar-looking words.

Arrange in correct logical order:
A: The men wore plain linen collars, jerkins and breeches, and kept their hair short.
B: The Royalists continued to wear elegant satins, plumed hats and long fashionable hair.
C: In the middle of the seventeenth century, clothing became a sign of political allegiance.
D: The Parliamentary forces, who were Puritans, adopted very plain clothes.

questions.text:
[Context passage — read before answering]
There was considerable concern expressed by biochemists in 2002 when news emerged that traces of acrylamide were found in a great many foodstuffs, including daily basics such as bread, breakfast cereals and potato and cheese products. Acrylamide is widely found in many processed foods but it is also created during home cooking, in fact whenever sugar, found in so many foods, is heated and browns. Blood tests showed that it was present in high concentrations in the vast majority of people in the Western world. The few animal studies that had taken place suggested that acrylamide is a carcinogen, but little was known about the toxicological consequences of ingesting the chemical daily and at the levels found in the general population. An urgent search began to establish what risk it posed to public health and how it might be removed from or reduced in food.

[Question]
The author of the passage would most likely disagree that many of the questions posed in the passage are now close to being answered.
